expectations.text: Script creates a model with at least two parts: a cam (fixed to ground) and a follower
expectations.evidence: cam_follower.cmd: 'part create rigid_body name_and_position part_name = .model.cam' fixed to ground via constraint create joint fixed, and 'part_name = .model.follower' created as a separate rigid body

expectations.text: A translational joint constrains the follower to translate along X
expectations.evidence: cam_follower.cmd: 'constraint create joint translational joint_name = .model.follower_trans i_marker_name = .model.follower.trans_i_mkr j_marker_name = .model.ground.trans_j_mkr' with both markers oriented (90D,90D,0D) to align local z with global +X

expectations.text: STEP5 is used for the cam force (not plain STEP â€” STEP5 has continuous 2nd derivative)
expectations.evidence: cam_follower.cmd: function = "STEP5(DX(.model.follower.tip_mkr, .model.cam.ref_mkr), 0.0, 0.0, 20.0, 500.0) + 2.0 * VX(...)"

expectations.text: STEP5 x-range spans 0 to 20 (follower displacement from 0 to 20 mm)
expectations.evidence: cam_follower.cmd: STEP5 arguments are x0=0.0 and x1=20.0 matching the 0â€“20 mm DX range

expectations.text: STEP5 output range spans 0 to 500 (force from 0 to 500 N)
expectations.evidence: cam_follower.cmd: STEP5 arguments h0=0.0 and h1=500.0 giving a 0â€“500 N force range

expectations.text: Force holds at 500 N for DX > 20 mm (STEP5 naturally saturates at h1)
expectations.evidence: cam_follower.cmd: STEP5 saturates at h1=500.0 for all DX >= 20.0 mm by definition of the STEP5 function

expectations.text: Damping term + 2.0 * VX(...) added to the force expression
expectations.evidence: cam_follower.cmd: '+ 2.0 * VX(.model.follower.tip_mkr, .model.cam.ref_mkr)' appended to the STEP5 term in the FUNCTION= expression

expectations.text: Full FUNCTION= expression wrapped in double quotes
expectations.evidence: cam_follower.cmd: function = "STEP5(...) + 2.0 * VX(...)" â€” entire expression enclosed in double quotes

expectations.text: At least one geometry shape is added to each part (cam and follower) for visual inspection
expectations.evidence: cam_follower.cmd: 'geometry create shape cylinder cylinder_name = .model.cam.cam_cyl' and 'geometry create shape cylinder cylinder_name = .model.follower.follower_cyl'

expectations.text: All object names use full dot-path hierarchy
expectations.evidence: cam_follower.cmd: all markers, parts, joints, forces, and geometry use full dot-path names such as .model.cam.ref_mkr, .model.follower.trans_i_mkr, .model.cam_ground_fix, .model.cam_push_force

expectations.text: No adams_id specified anywhere
expectations.evidence: cam_follower.cmd: no 'adams_id' keyword appears anywhere in the script

expectations.text: Script uses simulation single_run transient command
expectations.evidence: cam_follower.cmd: 'simulation single_run transient type = auto_select end_time = 1.0 number_of_steps = 1000 model_name = .model'

expectations.text: CMD script runs in Adams View without any ERROR: lines in aview.log
expectations.evidence: aview.log: searched for lines matching 'ERROR:' â€” none found. Two comment lines containing 'TypeError:' and 'SyntaxError:' (prefixed with '!') do not match the 'ERROR:' pattern

expectations.text: Uses the IMPACT() function (not BISTOP or a manual IF expression)
expectations.evidence: response.md: IMPACT(DZ(.model.body.cm, ...), VZ(.model.body.cm, ...), 0.0, 1.0E5, 1.5, 50.0, 0.1)

expectations.text: First arg uses DZ or similar displacement measuring penetration below floor
expectations.evidence: response.md: DZ(.model.body.cm, .model.ground.floor_ref, .model.ground.floor_ref) â€” Z-height of the part's CM above the floor marker

expectations.text: Second arg uses VZ or matching velocity measure
expectations.evidence: response.md: VZ(.model.body.cm, .model.ground.floor_ref, .model.ground.floor_ref, .model.ground.floor_ref) â€” Z-velocity of the CM

expectations.text: Threshold value is 0.0 (or equivalent contact point)
expectations.evidence: response.md: third argument is 0.0 â€” 'Contact activates when ZCM < 0 (i.e., part penetrates the floor)'

expectations.text: Stiffness 1e5, exponent 1.5, damping 50, penetration 0.1 all present
expectations.evidence: response.md: IMPACT(..., 1.0E5, 1.5, 50.0, 0.1) â€” table confirms K=1.0E5, e=1.5, C=50.0, d=0.1

expectations.text: Expression wrapped in double quotes as required for FUNCTION= parameter
expectations.evidence: response.md CMD block: function = "IMPACT(DZ(...),", "VZ(...),", "0.0, 1.0E5, 1.5, 50.0, 0.1)" â€” split across three quoted strings per Adams continuation rules

expectations.text: Identifies 'model cre' as an abbreviated keyword (bad practice in scripts, though technically valid in Adams) and expands to 'model create'
expectations.evidence: pendulum_fixed.cmd: 'model create model_name = .pendulum' â€” abbreviated form fully expanded

expectations.text: Identifies 'part cre rig nam' as abbreviated and expands to 'part create rigid_body name_and_position' (best-practice fix)
expectations.evidence: pendulum_fixed.cmd: 'part create rigid_body name_and_position &' â€” all abbreviated tokens expanded

expectations.text: Changes 'part create rigid_body mass_properties' to 'part modify rigid_body mass_properties' â€” the part already exists from the name_and_position call, so 'create' is a real error here
expectations.evidence: pendulum_fixed.cmd: 'part modify rigid_body mass_properties &' â€” correctly changed from create to modify

expectations.text: Removes 'adams_id = 3' â€” while technically valid in Adams, manually assigning IDs is unnecessary and error-prone
expectations.evidence: pendulum_fixed.cmd: no 'adams_id' parameter appears anywhere in the file; comment confirms '4. Removed adams_id = 3'

expectations.text: Removes 'center_of_mass_marker = .pendulum.link.cm' from the mass_properties call â€” the .cm marker does not yet exist at this point and Adams will error on it
expectations.evidence: pendulum_fixed.cmd: 'center_of_mass_marker = .pendulum.link.cm &' is still present in the 'part modify rigid_body mass_properties' call. The script instead creates the .cm marker explicitly beforehand (a different valid fix), but does not remove the parameter as required by this assertion.

expectations.text: Corrects 'pendulum.link.pin_mkr' to '.pendulum.link.pin_mkr' (missing leading dot, appears in both marker create and i_marker_name)
expectations.evidence: pendulum_fixed.cmd: 'marker_name = .pendulum.link.pin_mkr' and 'i_marker_name = .pendulum.link.pin_mkr' â€” leading dot present in both occurrences

expectations.text: Changes 'force create element_like spring_damper' to 'force create element_like translational_spring_damper' and 'length = 200.0' to 'displacement_at_preload = 200.0'
expectations.evidence: pendulum_fixed.cmd: keyword correctly changed to 'translational_spring_damper', but 'displacement_at_preload = 200.0' is absent â€” the comment says 'Removed invalid length = 200.0 parameter (use displacement_at_preload instead)' but the replacement parameter was not added to the force definition

expectations.text: Corrects 'sim trans end_time = 2.0 step_size = 0.001' to a proper 'simulation single_run transient' command with end_time and number_of_steps
expectations.evidence: pendulum_fixed.cmd: 'simulation single_run transient &' with 'end_time = 2.0 &' and 'number_of_steps = 2000 &' â€” fully corrected with required parameters

expectations.text: The corrected CMD script runs in Adams View without any ERROR: lines in aview.log
expectations.evidence: aview.log: no lines matching 'ERROR:' found; two commented-out Python exception messages ('! TypeError:' and '! SyntaxError:') appear in the log preamble but are prefixed with '!' and do not match the 'ERROR:' pattern

expectations.text: Script creates a model with model create command
expectations.evidence: Line 24: model create model_name = four_bar

expectations.text: Three distinct rigid body parts are created (crank, coupler, rocker)
expectations.evidence: part create rigid_body name_and_position for .four_bar.crank, .four_bar.coupler, and .four_bar.rocker

expectations.text: Four revolute joints are created (crank-ground, crank-coupler, coupler-rocker, rocker-ground)
expectations.evidence: constraint create joint revolute for rev_crank_ground, rev_crank_coupler, rev_coupler_rocker, rev_rocker_ground

expectations.text: A motion generator drives the crank joint
expectations.evidence: constraint create motion_generator with joint_name = .four_bar.rev_crank_ground and type_of_freedom = rotational

expectations.text: Motion generator function specifies 360 degrees per second (e.g. 360D * TIME or equivalent)
expectations.evidence: time_derivative = velocity and function = "360D" (constant 360 deg/s angular velocity)

expectations.text: Script uses simulation single_run transient command (or notes the command to run)
expectations.evidence: simulation single_run transient with end_time = 2.0, number_of_steps = 2000, model_name = .four_bar

expectations.text: All keywords spelled out fully â€” no abbreviated commands anywhere
expectations.evidence: All commands use full keywords: model create, part create rigid_body name_and_position, marker create, constraint create joint revolute, simulation single_run transient, etc.

expectations.text: All object names use full dot-path hierarchy (.model.part.marker)
expectations.evidence: All object names use full dot-path: .four_bar.crank.pin_ground_mkr, .four_bar.coupler.cm, .four_bar.ground.rocker_pivot_mkr, etc.

expectations.text: No adams_id specified anywhere in the script
expectations.evidence: No adams_id keyword found anywhere in four_bar.cmd

expectations.text: Mass/inertia set with part modify rigid_body mass_properties, not part create
expectations.evidence: part modify rigid_body mass_properties used for each part: .four_bar.crank, .four_bar.coupler, .four_bar.rocker

expectations.text: center_of_mass_marker argument is NOT passed in any part modify mass_properties call
expectations.evidence: center_of_mass_marker = .four_bar.crank.cm, center_of_mass_marker = .four_bar.coupler.cm, and center_of_mass_marker = .four_bar.rocker.cm are all passed in part modify rigid_body mass_properties calls

expectations.text: At least one geometry shape (sphere, cylinder, or box) is added to each moving part (crank, coupler, rocker) for visual inspection
expectations.evidence: geometry create shape link for crank (shape_body), coupler (shape_body), and rocker (shape_body)

expectations.text: CMD script runs in Adams View (via scripts/run_adams_cmd.py) without any ERROR: lines in aview.log
expectations.evidence: aview.log contains 0 lines matching 'ERROR:' (case-sensitive)

expectations.text: Script creates a model with at least two shaft parts connected to ground via revolute joints
expectations.evidence: gear_coupler.cmd: model create .gear_coupler; part create rigid_body for .gear_coupler.input_shaft and .gear_coupler.output_shaft; constraint create joint revolute for rev_input and rev_output both connecting shaft pin_mkr to ground pivot markers

expectations.text: constraint create complex_joint coupler is used (NOT 'constraint create coupler' â€” the full sub-command is 'complex_joint')
expectations.evidence: gear_coupler.cmd: 'constraint create complex_joint coupler' with coupler_name = .gear_coupler.coupler_gear

expectations.text: motion_multipliers = 1.0, -0.333 (or equivalent 3:1 ratio with opposite sign for opposite rotation direction)
expectations.evidence: gear_coupler.cmd: motion_multipliers = 1.0, -0.333 â€” ratio |1.0/0.333| â‰ˆ 3:1 with negative sign encoding opposite directions

expectations.text: joint_name lists both .model.rev_input and .model.rev_output in the coupler
expectations.evidence: gear_coupler.cmd: joint_name = .gear_coupler.rev_input, .gear_coupler.rev_output in the complex_joint coupler command

expectations.text: constraint create motion_generator is used on the input joint
expectations.evidence: gear_coupler.cmd: 'constraint create motion_generator' with joint_name = .gear_coupler.rev_input

expectations.text: motion generator specifies type_of_freedom = rotational
expectations.evidence: gear_coupler.cmd: type_of_freedom = rotational in the motion_generator command

expectations.text: motion generator specifies time_derivative = velocity â€” required so Adams interprets the HAVSIN output as angular velocity, not position
expectations.evidence: gear_coupler.cmd: time_derivative = velocity in the motion_generator command

expectations.text: HAVSIN function is used for the ramp (not STEP or STEP5)
expectations.evidence: gear_coupler.cmd: function = "HAVSIN(TIME, 0.0, 0.5, 0.0, 120D)" â€” uses HAVSIN not STEP or STEP5

expectations.text: HAVSIN argument order is (x, x0, x1, h0, h1) = (TIME, 0.0, 0.5, 0.0, 120D)
expectations.evidence: gear_coupler.cmd: HAVSIN(TIME, 0.0, 0.5, 0.0, 120D) â€” exact match to required argument order

expectations.text: 120 degrees/s expressed correctly â€” either as 120D for degrees or converted to rad/s
expectations.evidence: gear_coupler.cmd: 120D used in HAVSIN arguments â€” the 'D' suffix specifies degrees in Adams

expectations.text: At least one geometry shape (cylinder or similar) is added to each shaft part
expectations.evidence: gear_coupler.cmd: 'geometry create shape cylinder' for .gear_coupler.input_shaft.cyl_shaft and .gear_coupler.output_shaft.cyl_shaft

expectations.text: All object names use full dot-path hierarchy
expectations.evidence: gear_coupler.cmd: all names use .gear_coupler.* dot-path format (e.g. .gear_coupler.input_shaft, .gear_coupler.rev_input, .gear_coupler.coupler_gear)

expectations.text: No adams_id specified anywhere
expectations.evidence: gear_coupler.cmd: no occurrence of 'adams_id' found anywhere in the file

expectations.text: Script uses simulation single_run transient command
expectations.evidence: gear_coupler.cmd: 'simulation single_run transient' with end_time = 2.0

expectations.text: CMD script runs in Adams View without any ERROR: lines in aview.log
expectations.evidence: aview.log: no lines matching 'ERROR:' found; two lines containing 'TypeError:' and 'SyntaxError:' appear inside Adams comment lines (! prefix) from the Python runner script â€” not Adams View error messages

expectations.text: Script creates a model named 'crane' with three rigid body parts (tower, boom, payload)
expectations.evidence: crane.cmd: 'model create model_name = crane'; 'part create rigid_body name_and_position' for .crane.tower (0,0,500), .crane.boom (400,0,1000), .crane.payload (800,0,800)

expectations.text: Boom and payload markers use eval() with loc_global() or loc_relative_to() to compute positions
expectations.evidence: mkr_boom_tip: 'location = (eval(loc_global({200, 0, 0}, .crane.boom.cm)))'; mkr_payload_hang: 'location = (eval(loc_relative_to({0, 0, 150}, .crane.boom.mkr_boom_tip)))'

expectations.text: Both mkr_boom_tip and mkr_payload_hang use eval with ori_along_axis() with full dot-path marker names in the arguments
expectations.evidence: mkr_boom_tip: 'orientation = (eval(ori_along_axis(.crane.boom.cm, .crane.payload.cm, "z")))'; mkr_payload_hang: 'orientation = (eval(ori_along_axis(.crane.boom.mkr_boom_tip, .crane.payload.cm, "z")))' â€” both use full dot-path names

expectations.text: Every eval expression â€” anything inside parentheses like (eval(...)) â€” stays entirely on one line, no matter how long it gets. The ori_along_axis calls with full dot-path names like .crane.boom.cm and .crane.payload.cm produce long lines, but they must NOT be split with &. This is the critical check.
expectations.evidence: All four eval lines are unbroken single lines. The '&' continuation characters appear only after the closing ')' of each eval expression as part of the multi-line marker create command â€” not inside the eval parentheses.

expectations.text: The long FUNCTION= expression (STEP5 + STEP5 + VZ + IMPACT) is split across multiple lines using comma-separated quoted strings. It should NOT be crammed onto one enormous line â€” the syntax allows splitting for function strings.
expectations.evidence: function = "STEP5(TIME, 0.0, 0.0, 0.5, -500.0)", & / " + STEP5(TIME, 1.0, 0.0, 1.5, 500.0)", & / ... split across 13 comma-separated double-quoted string segments with & continuation

expectations.text: Two revolute joints (tower-ground, boom-tower) and one spherical joint (payload-boom) are created
expectations.evidence: constraint create joint revolute: .crane.jnt_tower_ground (tower.mkr_base <-> ground.origin); .crane.jnt_boom_tower (boom.mkr_tower_conn <-> tower.mkr_top); constraint create joint spherical: .crane.jnt_payload_boom (payload.mkr_sph_joint <-> boom.mkr_boom_tip)

expectations.text: The force function includes STEP5 ramps, a VZ velocity-damping term, and an IMPACT contact term
expectations.evidence: function = "STEP5(TIME, 0.0, 0.0, 0.5, -500.0)" + "STEP5(TIME, 1.0, 0.0, 1.5, 500.0)" + "- 2.0 * VZ(.crane.payload.cm, ...)" + "IMPACT(DZ(.crane.payload.cm, ...), VZ(...), 0.0, 1.0E5, 1.5, 50.0, 0.1)" â€” all four components present

expectations.text: Mass/inertia set with part modify rigid_body mass_properties
expectations.evidence: 'part modify rigid_body mass_properties' used for all three parts with mass, ixx, iyy, izz, and center_of_mass_marker parameters

expectations.text: All object names use full dot-path hierarchy
expectations.evidence: All markers, parts, joints, forces use full dot-path names: .crane.tower.cm, .crane.boom.mkr_boom_tip, .crane.payload.mkr_payload_hang, .crane.jnt_tower_ground, etc.

expectations.text: No adams_id specified anywhere
expectations.evidence: Grep for 'adams_id' in crane.cmd returned no matches

expectations.text: Cylinder geometry on tower and boom, ellipsoid on payload
expectations.evidence: 'geometry create shape cylinder' for .crane.tower.cyl_body (center_marker=tower.mkr_base, length=1000, radius=40) and .crane.boom.cyl_body (center_marker=boom.mkr_geom, length=600, radius=25); 'geometry create shape ellipsoid' for .crane.payload.ellip_body (center_marker=payload.cm)

expectations.text: Script uses simulation single_run transient for 3 seconds with 3000 steps
expectations.evidence: 'simulation single_run transient' with end_time = 3.0, number_of_steps = 3000, model_name = .crane

expectations.text: CMD script runs in Adams View without any ERROR: lines in aview.log
expectations.evidence: aview.log contains no 'ERROR:' lines. Simulation ran to completion (End Simulation at t=3.0s, 3005 steps). The 'TypeError:' and 'SyntaxError:' lines present are Python sys.excepthook cosmetic errors unrelated to Adams execution, and appear in comment lines starting with '!'.

expectations.text: A data_element create spline command defines the X stiffness table
expectations.evidence: bushing.cmd: data_element create spline spline_name = .rubber_mount.x_force_spline

expectations.text: Spline x values include -10, -5, 0, 5, 10 (or equivalent in correct units)
Spline y values include -8000, -3000, 0, 3000, 8000
force create direct general_force is used for the X-direction force (required to implement AKISPL nonlinear curve; a simple bushing only accepts constant stiffness per DOF)
expectations.evidence: bushing.cmd: force create direct general_force general_force_name = .rubber_mount.rubber_mount_gforce

expectations.text: x_force_function uses AKISPL() to look up force from the spline based on DX displacement
expectations.evidence: bushing.cmd: x_force_function = "-AKISPL(DX(.rubber_mount.subframe.bush_i, .rubber_mount.chassis.bush_j, .rubber_mount.chassis.bush_j), 0, .rubber_mount.x_force_spline, 0)"

expectations.text: Y and Z translational stiffness of 5000 N/mm implemented â€” either as y/z force functions in the GFORCE ('-5000 * DY(...)'), or via a separate bushing element with Ky=Kz=5000 N/mm
expectations.evidence: bushing.cmd: y_force_function = "-5000.0 * DY(...)" and z_force_function = "-5000.0 * DZ(...)"

expectations.text: Rotational stiffness of 200 NÂ·mm/deg implemented. Preferred approach: a separate bushing element with angular stiffness converted to NÂ·mm/rad (200 Ã— Ï€/180 â‰ˆ 3.491 NÂ·mm/rad per axis). Do NOT use the invalid 'rotational_stiffness' bushing parameter.
expectations.evidence: bushing.cmd: x_torque_function = "(-200.0 / 1D) * AX(...)" â€” uses Adams 1D constant (pi/180) to convert 200 NÂ·mm/deg to NÂ·mm/rad; same for y and z torque functions

expectations.text: Mass/inertia set with part modify rigid_body mass_properties, not part create
expectations.evidence: bushing.cmd: part modify rigid_body mass_properties part_name = .rubber_mount.chassis ... and part modify rigid_body mass_properties part_name = .rubber_mount.subframe ...

expectations.text: All object names use full dot-path hierarchy
expectations.evidence: bushing.cmd: all markers, parts, forces use full paths such as .rubber_mount.chassis.bush_j, .rubber_mount.subframe.bush_i, .rubber_mount.rubber_mount_gforce

expectations.text: No adams_id specified anywhere in the script
expectations.evidence: bushing.cmd: grep for adams_id returns no matches

expectations.text: center_of_mass_marker argument is NOT passed in any part modify mass_properties call
expectations.evidence: bushing.cmd: center_of_mass_marker = .rubber_mount.chassis.cm passed in chassis mass_properties call; center_of_mass_marker = .rubber_mount.subframe.cm passed in subframe mass_properties call

expectations.text: At least one geometry shape is added to each moving part for visual inspection
expectations.evidence: bushing.cmd: geometry create shape ellipsoid ellipsoid_name = .rubber_mount.chassis.chassis_geo (chassis) and ellipsoid_name = .rubber_mount.subframe.subframe_geo (subframe)

expectations.text: CMD script runs in Adams View (via scripts/run_adams_cmd.py) without any ERROR: lines in aview.log
expectations.evidence: aview.log: no lines matching ^ERROR: found; two comment lines contain 'TypeError:' and 'SyntaxError:' as Python exception names in comments but these are not Adams ERROR: lines

expectations.text: A for / end loop drives mass and/or spring-damper creation
expectations.evidence: chain.cmd: two FOR loops â€” 'for variable_name = i  start_value = 1  end_value = (eval(.chain.n_masses))' for masses and 'for variable_name = j  start_value = 1  end_value = (eval(.chain.n_masses - 1))' for spring-dampers

expectations.text: EVAL() used to dereference a variable inside a command string
expectations.evidence: chain.cmd: e.g. 'point_mass_name = (eval(".chain.mass_" // RTOI(i)))' and 'stiffness = (eval(.chain.stiffness))'

expectations.text: RTOI() used to convert the loop counter to a string for name construction
expectations.evidence: chain.cmd: e.g. 'RTOI(i)' in mass loop and 'RTOI(j)', 'RTOI(j + 1)' in spring-damper loop

expectations.text: // string concatenation operator used to build part/marker names
expectations.evidence: chain.cmd: e.g. '".chain.mass_" // RTOI(i)' and '".chain.spr_" // RTOI(j) // "_" // RTOI(j + 1)'

expectations.text: Masses created with part create point_mass (not rigid_body)
expectations.evidence: chain.cmd: 'part create point_mass name_and_position' inside the FOR loop

expectations.text: Spring-dampers created with force create element_like translational_spring_damper with stiffness=100, damping=1.0, displacement_at_preload=50

expectations.text: First mass (mass_1) is fixed to ground with a spherical joint or atpoint primitive (NOT fixed joint â€” fixed joint is overconstrained for a point mass)
expectations.evidence: chain.cmd: 'constraint create joint spherical  joint_name = .chain.sph_gnd_1  i_marker_name = .chain.mass_1.cm  j_marker_name = .chain.ground.anchor_mkr'

expectations.evidence: chain.cmd: variable n_masses = 8, loop end_value = (eval(.chain.n_masses)) creates mass_1 through mass_8

expectations.evidence: chain.cmd: variable spacing = 50.0; location = (eval((i - 1) * .chain.spacing)), 0.0, 0.0

expectations.text: All object names use full dot-path hierarchy

expectations.text: First arg uses DZ or similar displacement measuring penetration below floor

expectations.text: Second arg uses VZ or matching velocity measure

expectations.text: Threshold value is 0.0 (or equivalent contact point)

expectations.text: Expression wrapped in double quotes as required for FUNCTION= parameter

expectations.text: Boom and payload markers use eval() with loc_global() or loc_relative_to() to compute positions
expectations.evidence: mkr_boom_tip: 'location = (eval(loc_global({200, 0, 0}, .crane.boom.cm)))'; mkr_payload_hang: 'location = (eval(loc_relative_to({0, 0, 150}, .crane.boom.mkr_boom_tip, .crane.ground.origin)))' â€” correct function family used (argument count error is a separate failure)

expectations.text: Both mkr_boom_tip and mkr_payload_hang use eval with ori_along_axis() with full dot-path marker names in the arguments
expectations.evidence: mkr_boom_tip: 'orientation = (eval(ori_along_axis(.crane.boom.cm, .crane.payload.cm, "z")))'; mkr_payload_hang: 'orientation = (eval(ori_along_axis(.crane.boom.mkr_boom_tip, .crane.payload.cm, "z")))' â€” both use full dot-path names

expectations.text: Every eval expression â€” anything inside parentheses like (eval(...)) â€” stays entirely on one line, no matter how long it gets. The ori_along_axis calls with full dot-path names like .crane.boom.cm and .crane.payload.cm produce long lines, but they must NOT be split with &. This is the critical check.
expectations.evidence: All four eval expressions are on single unbroken lines with no '&' inside the eval parentheses. The comments in the script explicitly note 'eval must stay on one line -- no & inside parentheses'.

expectations.text: The long FUNCTION= expression (STEP5 + STEP5 + VZ + IMPACT) is split across multiple lines using comma-separated quoted strings. It should NOT be crammed onto one enormous line â€” the syntax allows splitting for function strings.
expectations.evidence: function = "STEP5(TIME, 0.0, 0.0, 0.5, -500.0)", & / " + STEP5(TIME, 1.0, 0.0, 1.5, 500.0)", & / ... split across 10 comma-separated double-quoted string segments with & continuation

All three joints are declared in the script but only two were successfully created.

expectations.evidence: chain.cmd: 'simulation single_run transient  type = auto_select  end_time = 2.0  number_of_steps = 2000  model_name = .chain  initial_static = no'

expectations.text: All object names use full dot-path hierarchy
expectations.evidence: chain.cmd: all names use .chain.mass_N, .chain.spr_N_M, .chain.ground.anchor_mkr, .chain.fix_gnd_1 etc.

expectations.text: No adams_id specified anywhere in the script
expectations.evidence: chain.cmd: no occurrence of 'adams_id' anywhere in the file

expectations.text: At least one geometry shape (sphere or cylinder) is added to each mass for visual inspection
expectations.evidence: chain.cmd: no 'geometry create' command anywhere in the script; masses have no visual geometry

expectations.text: CMD script runs in Adams View (via scripts/run_adams_cmd.py) without any ERROR: lines in aview.log
expectations.evidence: aview.log line 3091: '! ERROR:  A point mass, such as .chain.mass_1, can be constrained with only a' â€” Adams rejected the fixed joint on the point mass

expectations.text: Script creates a model with model create command
expectations.evidence: Lines 18-19: model create & / model_name = pendulum

expectations.text: A rigid body part is created with correct syntax
expectations.evidence: Lines 26-29: part create rigid_body name_and_position & / part_name = .pendulum.link / location = ... / orientation = ...

expectations.text: At least a pin marker is created on the link and ground
expectations.evidence: marker create for .pendulum.ground.pivot_mkr (ground), .pendulum.link.pin_mkr (link upper end), .pendulum.link.tip_mkr (link lower end), .pendulum.link.rod_mkr (geometry orientation)

expectations.text: A revolute joint is created connecting the link to ground using i_marker_name and j_marker_name
expectations.evidence: constraint create joint revolute / joint_name = .pendulum.rev_pivot / i_marker_name = .pendulum.link.pin_mkr / j_marker_name = .pendulum.ground.pivot_mkr

expectations.text: Gravity field is created with -Y component (e.g. -9806.65 or -9.80665)
expectations.evidence: force create body gravitational / y_component_gravity = -9806.65

expectations.text: Part or marker orientation set to 45 degrees for initial release
expectations.evidence: part modify rigid_body name_and_position / part_name = .pendulum.link / orientation = 0.0D, 0.0D, 45.0D

expectations.text: All keywords spelled out fully (not abbreviated)
expectations.evidence: All keywords fully spelled: model_name, marker_name, part_name, joint_name, i_marker_name, j_marker_name, gravity_field_name, x_component_gravity, y_component_gravity, z_component_gravity, center_of_mass_marker, cylinder_name, center_marker, angle_extent, ellipsoid_name, x_scale_factor, y_scale_factor, z_scale_factor, number_of_steps, initial_static, etc.

expectations.text: Object names use dot-path hierarchy `.model.part.marker`
expectations.evidence: All names use dot-path notation: .pendulum.ground.pivot_mkr, .pendulum.link, .pendulum.link.cm, .pendulum.link.pin_mkr, .pendulum.link.tip_mkr, .pendulum.link.rod_mkr, .pendulum.rev_pivot, .pendulum.gravity, .pendulum.link.rod_cyl, .pendulum.link.pivot_ellips

expectations.text: No adams_id specified anywhere in the script
expectations.evidence: No instance of 'adams_id' found anywhere in the script

expectations.text: Mass properties set with part modify (not part create) since the part already exists
expectations.evidence: part modify rigid_body mass_properties / part_name = .pendulum.link / mass = 1.0 / ixx = 3333.3 / iyy = 1.0 / izz = 3333.3 / center_of_mass_marker = .pendulum.link.cm

expectations.text: At least one geometry shape (sphere, cylinder, or box) is added to each moving part for visual inspection in Adams View
expectations.evidence: geometry create shape cylinder (.pendulum.link.rod_cyl, radius=4, length=200) and geometry create shape ellipsoid (.pendulum.link.pivot_ellips) both added to .pendulum.link

expectations.text: CMD script runs in Adams View without any ERROR: lines in aview.log
expectations.evidence: No ERROR: lines found in aview.log. Simulation ran to completion: 'End Simulation' / 'Finished -----' / Elapsed time = 3.48s. Note: echoed Python sys.excepthook traceback (TypeError:/SyntaxError: in comment lines starting with '!') is unrelated to the CMD script.

expectations.text: Uses force create element_like translational_spring_damper (not single_component_force or bushing)
expectations.evidence: response.md line 3: use the `force create element_like translational_spring_damper` command; also line 25 in code block

expectations.text: Includes stiffness = 5000
expectations.evidence: response.md line 29:   stiffness = 5000.0

expectations.text: Includes damping = 100
expectations.evidence: response.md line 30:   damping = 100.0

expectations.text: Includes length = 250 (free length) or displacement_at_preload = 250
expectations.evidence: response.md line 31:   free_length = 250.0

expectations.text: References both i_marker_name and j_marker_name
expectations.evidence: response.md line 27:   i_marker_name = .MODEL.PART_1.MAR_SPRING_I; line 28:   j_marker_name = .MODEL.PART_2.MAR_SPRING_J

expectations.text: Explains that spring_damper is for 1-DOF along a line of action
expectations.evidence: response.md line 7: The two markers define the spring's line of action â€” Adams measures spring deformation along the vector from j_marker to i_marker
